France Hits the Demographic Point of No Return || Peter Zeihan

The example of Paris shows that policies can make a huge difference. Until 1970 the population of France continued to move to Paris en masse. It was projected that the Paris region might grow to 16 million people by 2000. In the 1970s, the French governmnet made efforts to de-centralize the country, and Paris stopped to outgrow the country.
France's population decline presents a political challenge
UK vs France - 300 years of population change in one glance

🇬🇧🇫🇷 UK vs France - 300 years of population change in one glance!

From the 1700s to 2025, these two neighbours followed very different demographic paths.

📜 Then
• 1710:
UK - 7.3M
France - 22.5M

France was already a demographic giant while Britain was still a small island nation.

🏭 Industrial boom
• 1800–1900:
The UK explodes from 10.8M → 41M
France grows more slowly from 29M → 40M

🇬🇧 Britain overtakes France around 1900.

👶 Fertility collapse
Throughout the 20th century both countries saw fertility fall below replacement level - especially the UK after 1950.

🌍 What keeps them growing today?
2025 estimates:
• UK - 69.5M (fertility 1.39)
  ➕ +8.28M net migration since 1990
  ➕ +4.4M natural growth

• France - 68.6M (fertility 1.58)
  ➕ +3.2M net migration since 1990
  ➕ +6.64M natural growth

➡️ The UK now relies much more on immigration, while France still gets more growth from births.

📉 Both countries are aging fast - and without migration, population decline would already be re
France Records First Peacetime Natural Population Decline As Deaths Exceed Births

Despite deaths outnumbering births, France's population grew slightly last year to 69.1 million, due to net migration, which INSEE estimated at 176,000. 
Life expectancy reached record highs last year - 85.9 years for women and 80.3 for men - while the share of people aged 65 or older climbed to 22%, nearly matching those under 20.

FRANCE 24 English. . 🇫🇷 🔎 For the first time since World War II, the number of deaths recorded in #France has exceeded the number of births recorded in the year 2025. This change could have wider effects on the French #economy and #healthcare ecosyste

In fact the population of the Russian Empire in 1805 could have been as low as 31.5M with 36M as the very highest possible point. This just shows how strong demographically France was in 1805 and how far they fell even without mass emigration.  By 1939 French population just~41M.
